title: Dedicated: per-tenant batching tier overrides + precedence rules
preview: Context: As part of tiered batching defaults (fast/standard/cost-efficient), Dedicated customers need an explicit override mechanism with deterministic precedence across (a) cluster-level config, (b) tenant overrides, (c) route/model policies, and (d) runtime defaults. We also need to make the effective tier/knob se...
text:
Context: As part of tiered batching defaults (fast/standard/cost-efficient), Dedicated customers need an explicit override mechanism with deterministic precedence across (a) cluster-level config, (b) tenant overrides, (c) route/model policies, and (d) runtime defaults. We also need to make the effective tier/knob set explainable in telemetry (reason codes) and avoid surprises during staged rollout.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a Dedicated override surface that allows setting an explicit batching tier and/or explicit scheduler knobs for a tenant (optionally scoped to a route). 2) Implements precedence resolution in the policy engine + runtime config adapter so the effective profile is stable and debuggable: (highest) tenant override > route policy tier > tier default (model/hardware) > legacy batching fields (only when tier feature flag off) > compiled runtime defaults (lowest). 3) Enfo...

title: Feature flag + staged rollout controls for tiered batching defaults
preview: Summary This PR adds feature flags, staged rollout configuration, and a kill switch for enabling tiered batching defaults safely. This is plumbing only; it does not change the scheduler heuristics or knob values (those land in separate PRs). Motivation Tiered batching defaults will be rolled out gradually (internal ...
text: Summary This PR adds feature flags, staged rollout configuration, and a kill switch for enabling tiered batching defaults safely. This is plumbing only; it does not change the scheduler heuristics or knob values (those land in separate PRs). Motivation Tiered batching defaults will be rolled out gradually (internal canary → hosted subset → Dedicated opt-in). We need: (1) a single global gate, (2) a staged rollout mechanism that can be tightened to an allowlist quickly, and (3) an emergency kill switch that reverts to legacy behavior immediately when p99/queueing regressions appear. This PR implements those control-plane/runtime toggles and the precedence rules. Scope / What changed Runtime feature flag + rollout policy Added a top-level gate TieredBatchingDefaults that controls whether the scheduler will attempt to select a tiered batching profile at all. When disabled, scheduler retu...

title: Handshake escrow and client-abort semantics for streaming sessions (OpenAI compatibility)
preview: Motivation: intermittent client disconnects during long-running streaming responses cause two classes of problems: 1) clients that reconnect within a short window are unable to resume because the server immediately tears down session state; 2) client-initiated cancels (or network-triggered resets) have ambiguous sem...
text: Motivation: intermittent client disconnects during long-running streaming responses cause two classes of problems: 1) clients that reconnect within a short window are unable to resume because the server immediately tears down session state; 2) client-initiated cancels (or network-triggered resets) have ambiguous semantics with respect to tool/function call idempotency and structured output flushing. This PR introduces a handshake escrow token and clarified client-abort semantics to make resume/reconnect and abort behavior deterministic and OpenAI-compatible. Key changes: - Introduce a short-lived session escrow ticket (Escrow-Token) retained server-side for a configurable grace window to support reconnect-and-resume for active stream sessions. The token is returned in initial stream response headers and via the OpenAI-compat protocol extension. - Define deterministic semantics for cli...

title: Autorehearse promote controller with multi‑arm safety credits
preview: Motivation: Reduce risky promotes by exercising candidate variants against a rehearsal harness before any traffic cutover. This PR introduces an 'autorehearse' step in the promote controller that replays sampled requests against candidate model variants (dry-run) to estimate latency/correctness regressions and compu...
text: Motivation: Reduce risky promotes by exercising candidate variants against a rehearsal harness before any traffic cutover. This PR introduces an 'autorehearse' step in the promote controller that replays sampled requests against candidate model variants (dry-run) to estimate latency/correctness regressions and compute a multi-arm safety credit score. The safety credits act as a conservative rollback budget used by the promote planner to decide whether to advance, hold, or abort. Key features: 1) Rehearsal harness that can run lightweight replay using prefix-sampled traffic (configurable reservoir). 2) Per-arm safety credit calculation based on latency P95, error delta, and fidelity checks against golden prompts. 3) Integration with the rollout planner to gate phased promotes when credits fall below thresholds. 4) Rollback ledger and explainable advisor logs that record the reason for ...

title: adaptive batch scaling: seq-length-aware bucket resizing + padding-reduction heuristics
preview: Motivation: Mixed-sequence-length workloads (chat + long-form) suffer from padding waste and suboptimal GPU kernel selection when using fixed buckets. This change implements an adaptive bucket-resizing heuristic that observes recent sequence length distributions per route and dynamically resizes or merges buckets to...
text: Motivation: Mixed-sequence-length workloads (chat + long-form) suffer from padding waste and suboptimal GPU kernel selection when using fixed buckets. This change implements an adaptive bucket-resizing heuristic that observes recent sequence length distributions per route and dynamically resizes or merges buckets to minimize average padding while keeping latency SLOs. It also adds padding-aware scheduling that prefers coalescing requests whose effective token length (after prefix/KV reuse) aligns with available kernel tiling sizes. Key features: 1) Online bucket profiler: collects a lightweight histogram of observed input lengths per route over a sliding window and computes an optimal bucket partitioning using a cost function that trades padding tokens vs. queueing delay. 2) Adaptive resize policy: buckets can split, merge, or be retired based on utilization thresholds; changes are gr...

title: adaptive-signal-attenuator-and-stepwise-playbook-generator
preview: Motivation: On-call noise and low-signal alerts keep interrupting responders and dilute attention away from high-impact incidents. This PR introduces two complementary features: (1) an adaptive signal attenuator that temporarily mutes or down-prioritizes low-signal alert bursts based on multivariate heuristics (enti...
text: Motivation: On-call noise and low-signal alerts keep interrupting responders and dilute attention away from high-impact incidents. This PR introduces two complementary features: (1) an adaptive signal attenuator that temporarily mutes or down-prioritizes low-signal alert bursts based on multivariate heuristics (entity cardinality, recent noise history, error-rate delta), and (2) a stepwise playbook generator that converts canonical runbook templates into prioritized, step-by-step remediation checklists with embedded conditional telemetry checks. Both are exposed via the incident-bot core and a new CLI surface (oncallctl) and are designed for safe gradual rollout with canary attenuation and an explicit override flow.

title: add adaptive outlier suppression to canary evaluator
preview: Motivation: Canary evaluators occasionally trigger rollbacks due to short-lived outliers (e.g., transient downstream hiccups or probe skew) that do not reflect sustained regressions. This PR introduces an adaptive outlier suppression layer that reduces sensitivity to isolated spikes while preserving detection of rea...
text:
Motivation: Canary evaluators occasionally trigger rollbacks due to short-lived outliers (e.g., transient downstream hiccups or probe skew) that do not reflect sustained regressions. This PR introduces an adaptive outlier suppression layer that reduces sensitivity to isolated spikes while preserving detection of real regressions.

Summary of changes: Implemented a three-stage suppression strategy integrated into the existing canary scoring pipeline: 1) lightweight per-probe z-score normalization (robust to small sample sizes), 2) median-filtered spike-dampening that discounts single-sample deviations based on recent windowed variance, and 3) Bayesian weight recalibration that downweights probes with recurring discordant signals across slices. The suppression is parameterized and feature-flagged so operators can tune aggressiveness per-customer or per-route.

Why this is safe: The supp...

title: Optimize INT8 dequant-fusion kernel and smoothquant scheduling to reduce p99 and token cost
preview: Motivation: For the quantized inference stack we've been shipping two main bottlenecks: (1) the dequantization+matmul fusion path for INT8 kernels introduces extra memory traffic and kernel launches at each attention and FFN block; (2) SmoothQuant scaling interacts poorly with our scheduler and continuous-batching h...
text: Motivation: For the quantized inference stack we've been shipping two main bottlenecks: (1) the dequantization+matmul fusion path for INT8 kernels introduces extra memory traffic and kernel launches at each attention and FFN block; (2) SmoothQuant scaling interacts poorly with our scheduler and continuous-batching heuristics, causing variable p99 regressions under mixed-sequence workloads. This PR introduces three coordinated changes: 1) a fused dequantize+gemm kernel that accepts packed INT8 weights and produces FP16 accumulation with optional requantization hooks; 2) a scheduling tweak that biases smoothquant scaling application to the slower stage of the pipeline and batches compatible sequence shapes together more aggressively; 3) a lightweight KV-cache-aware fast path that skips per-step dequant for cached KV blocks and reuses FP16 cached keys when the model quantization profile ...

title: fragmented-kernel-workspace-pool-kv-rolling-reserve-and-adaptive-batching-prioritizer
preview: Motivation: Under heavy continuous-batching workloads we observe fragmented temporary workspace allocations and conservative KV cache reservations that inflate peak GPU memory by ~15-25%. This PR introduces a kernel-fragment pool allocator, a compacting fragment coalescer, a KV rolling-reserve policy with age-based ...
text: Motivation: Under heavy continuous-batching workloads we observe fragmented temporary workspace allocations and conservative KV cache reservations that inflate peak GPU memory by ~15-25%. This PR introduces a kernel-fragment pool allocator, a compacting fragment coalescer, a KV rolling-reserve policy with age-based demotion, and an adaptive batching prioritizer that protects tail latency while increasing packing efficiency. Goals: 1) reduce peak transient workspace (scratch) footprint, 2) lower KV cache reserved overhead for workloads with skewed hotsets, 3) keep 95p/99p latency equal or improved under steady-state continuous batching. Changes (high level): - runtime/worker: implement FragmentPool allocator that suballocates kernel workspace into variable-length fragments mapped to kernel affinity classes; supports fast reuse and best-fit coalescing on free. - runtime/scheduler: add A...

title: per-layer kernel occupancy probes + adaptive batch-window tracer (KV-stamp profiler)
preview: Summary: Instrumentation and microbench additions to help diagnose layer-level kernel occupancy and batch-window interactions with KV-cache. This PR introduces a lightweight per-layer occupancy probe, a stamp-based KV-cache sampler ("kv-stamp profiler"), and an adaptive batch-window tracer that records contiguous re...
text:
Summary: Instrumentation and microbench additions to help diagnose layer-level kernel occupancy and batch-window interactions with KV-cache. This PR introduces a lightweight per-layer occupancy probe, a stamp-based KV-cache sampler ("kv-stamp profiler"), and an adaptive batch-window tracer that records contiguous request grouping behavior used by the continuous-batching scheduler. All probes are zero-cost when disabled and are guarded behind runtime flags and RBAC-controlled debug endpoints.

Motivation and context: We observed tail-latency regressions under mixed request shapes where a few large layers dominated GPU occupancy and caused scheduler-induced head-of-line blocking. Existing coarse counters were insufficient to map slowdowns to individual kernel launches or to correlate KV-cache reuse with batch-window size. This work aims to give engineers the tools to: (1) attribute occu...

title: Real-time Runbook Compiler and Telemetry Stitcher
preview: Context and motivation: On-call responders frequently have to assemble fragmented signals (alerts, traces, logs, SLO deltas) into an actionable runbook under time pressure. This PR introduces a Runbook Compiler service that composes a compact, prioritized runbook from a set of incident signals and a Telemetry Stitch...
text:
Context and motivation: On-call responders frequently have to assemble fragmented signals (alerts, traces, logs, SLO deltas) into an actionable runbook under time pressure. This PR introduces a Runbook Compiler service that composes a compact, prioritized runbook from a set of incident signals and a Telemetry Stitcher that pulls and correlates relevant telemetry slices across services. The goal is to reduce TTR and make playbook suggestions reproducible and auditable.

What this PR does (summary):
- Add runbook-compiler microservice (go) that accepts an incident payload and returns a prioritized runbook (steps + rationale + safety gates).
- Add telemetry-stitcher worker (python) that builds correlated telemetry slices (spans, top traces, key logs, metric cutouts) and applies automatic sanitization rules to strip PII/keys.
- Add ephemeral-debug-proxy (node) that can safely replay a lim...

title: add-rolling-entropy-degradation-fence
preview: Summary: Introduces a Rolling Entropy Degradation Fence for canary rollouts. Motivation: percentile-based checks (p50/p95/p99) miss subtle distributional shifts where tails shift gradually or mass moves between latency modes. This PR implements an adaptive entropy-based detector that measures changes in the latency/...
text:
Summary: Introduces a Rolling Entropy Degradation Fence for canary rollouts. Motivation: percentile-based checks (p50/p95/p99) miss subtle distributional shifts where tails shift gradually or mass moves between latency modes. This PR implements an adaptive entropy-based detector that measures changes in the latency/throughput histogram distribution over a rolling window and fences rollouts when sustained distributional degradation is detected.

Design and behavior: The detector bins observed latency samples into an adaptive histogram (dynamic bucket sizing based on observed percentiles) and computes a rolling Shannon entropy plus a KL-divergence/JS-distance to a baseline histogram. We combine three signals: (1) instantaneous entropy change, (2) KL divergence to baseline, and (3) a sustained drift score computed with exponential decay. A canary fence is tripped only when the composite ...

title: Heterogeneous-bandwidth-aware allreduce fanout with KV-shard coalescing and comm/compute overlap
preview: Motivation: on mixed-cluster deployments we observed long tail latency and suboptimal throughput for tensor-parallel allreduce when nodes have heterogeneous NVLink/intra-node bandwidths or asymmetric cross-node links. Smaller microbatches and low-latency applications (streaming chat) disproportionately hit these tai...
text:
Motivation: on mixed-cluster deployments we observed long tail latency and suboptimal throughput for tensor-parallel allreduce when nodes have heterogeneous NVLink/intra-node bandwidths or asymmetric cross-node links. Smaller microbatches and low-latency applications (streaming chat) disproportionately hit these tails.

This PR introduces a bandwidth-aware fanout planner and a KV-shard coalescing pass that together reduce cross-node stalls and improve overlap between communication and compute. It also adds a narrow-copy optimized kernel for strided small-chunk merges and a runtime hook that maps comm streams to physical links based on a link-rate profile discovered at startup.

What changed (high level):
- Fanout planner: builds a minimal spanning fanout for allreduce that avoids slow links and increases per-hop concurrency when it detects bandwidth heterogeneity. The planner prefers ...

title: Smoother autoscaler signal and gateway backpressure for dedicated pools
preview: Summary: Introduces a smoothing layer in the Dedicated Autoscaler and a lightweight backpressure signal propagated from the gateway to fleet controllers. This reduces autoscaler thrash on spiky traffic and gives gateways a controlled way to shed load gracefully against per-pool soft capacity and throughput guarantee...
text:
Summary: Introduces a smoothing layer in the Dedicated Autoscaler and a lightweight backpressure signal propagated from the gateway to fleet controllers. This reduces autoscaler thrash on spiky traffic and gives gateways a controlled way to shed load gracefully against per-pool soft capacity and throughput guarantees.

Motivation: Customers on Dedicated have reported oscillations when short bursts cross autoscaler thresholds and then recede; this leads to unnecessary GPU spin-ups and billing variance, as well as transient error spikes. The gateway currently responds with 429s or fallback heuristics; we need a first-class, observable mechanism that cooperates with autoscaling and preserves headroom for SLA-critical requests.

Changes in this PR:
- Autoscaler: add Exponential Moving Average (EMA) smoothing of utilization and request rate with configurable half-life per-pool. New config ...

title: Multiplexer: parallel tool execution, idempotent retries, and ergonomic result injection
preview: Motivation: production workloads increasingly issue multiple tool calls per response (lookups, enrichment, external actions) and clients expect predictable injection semantics, low-latency parallelism, and transparent retry behavior when external tools fail. This PR introduces a lightweight multiplexer for tool exec...
text:
Motivation: production workloads increasingly issue multiple tool calls per response (lookups, enrichment, external actions) and clients expect predictable injection semantics, low-latency parallelism, and transparent retry behavior when external tools fail. This PR introduces a lightweight multiplexer for tool execution that: 1) executes independent tool calls in parallel when safe, 2) exposes an explicit idempotency token + retry middleware for transient tool failures, 3) provides an ergonomic result-injection API surface for handlers (deterministic injection points with ordering hints), and 4) keeps OpenAI-compatibility behaviors (function-calling fallback and structured outputs) intact.

Summary of changes: commits implement a runtime-level ToolMultiplexer that accepts a list of ToolCall requests and returns deterministic ToolResults with stable ids and insertion points. We added ...

title: bake signed artifact index and provenance into private-installer release manifests
preview: Motivation: private deployments (VPC/on-prem) require a reproducible, signed index that bundles release artifacts (installer tarballs, Helm charts, Terraform modules), attestations (SBOM/prov.json), and signature metadata so customers can verify what they deploy. This PR introduces a baked 'signed-artifact-index' ge...
text: Motivation: private deployments (VPC/on-prem) require a reproducible, signed index that bundles release artifacts (installer tarballs, Helm charts, Terraform modules), attestations (SBOM/prov.json), and signature metadata so customers can verify what they deploy. This PR introduces a baked 'signed-artifact-index' generation step in the private-installer release pipeline, integrates Notary v2-compatible signing (via GCP KMS in CI), surfaces provenance files alongside Helm and Terraform artifacts, and wires the installer/upgrade tooling to consume the index during installs and rollbacks. Implementation summary: 1) CI: add .github/workflows/release-private-installer-index.yml that builds artifacts in a hermetic container, generates SBOMs (syft), produces provenance JSON, and invokes notary-v2 client to produce signatures and an OCI index blob that contains pointers to signed artifacts; 2...

title: introduce-retry-bundles-and-stream-progress-telemetry-across-sdks
preview: Context: Customers have asked for a compact, declarative way to reuse retry semantics across SDKs and to receive lightweight progress telemetry for long-running streaming requests. This PR introduces (1) retry bundles — named retry/profile presets that can be referenced by "retryPolicy" on client config and (2) a st...
text: Context: Customers have asked for a compact, declarative way to reuse retry semantics across SDKs and to receive lightweight progress telemetry for long-running streaming requests. This PR introduces (1) retry bundles — named retry/profile presets that can be referenced by "retryPolicy" on client config and (2) a stream-progress telemetry envelope emitted by the SDKs for long-running pull/push streams. Motivation: reduce duplicated retry configuration in apps and enable observability consumers (SLO dashboards, SRE) to surface stream progress without instrumenting app-level callbacks. Summary of changes: added a retry bundle registry and lookup time resolution in the client core; exposed a small opt-in "stream_progress" event channel that SDKs can emit at stable intervals (configurable); implemented consistent semantics across Python/TypeScript/Go; added unit and integration tests and ...

title: Introduce token-lifecycle burnup histogram, cross-service latency fingerprint, and alert coalescer
preview: Context: token-level SLO noise and long-tail cost/latency alerts are creating alert fatigue. Existing token-cost rollups capture per-token aggregates but struggle to: (1) show how cost accumulates across pipeline stages (ingest, model, postprocess), (2) identify cross-service latency shape changes (same total latenc...
text:
Context: token-level SLO noise and long-tail cost/latency alerts are creating alert fatigue. Existing token-cost rollups capture per-token aggregates but struggle to: (1) show how cost accumulates across pipeline stages (ingest, model, postprocess), (2) identify cross-service latency shape changes (same total latency but different phase breakdown), and (3) suppress repeated similar alerts that indicate the same incident.

What this PR does: introduces three coordinated pieces:
1) token_lifecycle_burnup histogram metric (metric/token_lifecycle_burnup): records cumulative token cost/latency at stage boundaries per invocation (labels: tenant_id, model, route, stage). Supports configurable buckets and exported rollups.
2) latency_fingerprint: a small vectorized signature (string) derived from percentile deltas across stages for an invocation; stored as a tag on traces and emitted as a low...

title: KernelTide contention visualizer and probabilistic warmup coordinator for stability-focused bench suites
preview: Motivation: We've observed non-deterministic p95/p99 spikes in a subset of long-running throughput benchmarks that correlate with kernel-level contention and uneven warmup behavior across workers. This PR introduces two complementary components: (1) KernelTide — a contention visualizer that aggregates per-kernel occ...
text:
Motivation: We've observed non-deterministic p95/p99 spikes in a subset of long-running throughput benchmarks that correlate with kernel-level contention and uneven warmup behavior across workers. This PR introduces two complementary components: (1) KernelTide — a contention visualizer that aggregates per-kernel occupancy, stall-reasons and scheduling collisions into time-aligned heatmaps and compact contention-curves; (2) ProbabilisticWarmupCoordinator — a controller that runs randomized preflight primer passes across cohorts to probabilistically cover different KV cache and memory-placement states before measurement windows.

What this PR does (summary): Adds a lightweight sampling probe that annotates kernel events in the benchmark worker (Linux perf + runtime hooks), a postprocessor that synthesizes per-request kernel timelines into a compact parquet-friendly schema, an interactiv...

title: Add stratified noise calibrator and adaptive probe scheduler for low-signal canaries
preview: Context: Our canary detector has been producing noisy alerts for low-volume / heterogenous traffic slices (short queries, long queries, browser vs API) leading to unnecessary rollbacks. Motivation: reduce false positives and speed up meaningful detection in low-signal cohorts without increasing probe cost. This PR i...
text: Context: Our canary detector has been producing noisy alerts for low-volume / heterogenous traffic slices (short queries, long queries, browser vs API) leading to unnecessary rollbacks. Motivation: reduce false positives and speed up meaningful detection in low-signal cohorts without increasing probe cost. This PR introduces two complementary components: (1) Stratified Noise Calibrator (SNC) — a per-stratum robust noise estimator that derives baseline variance and Minimal Detectable Effect (MDE) using median-absolute-deviation + IQR trimming and an empirical noise model; and (2) Adaptive Probe Scheduler (APS) — a probe allocation layer that dynamically assigns probe budget to strata based on expected information gain (posterior variance reduction) and the user's rollout priority hints. Approach: SNC computes noise baselines per stratum (user_cohort, model_variant, sequence_length_buck...

title: Add latency-cliff detector and adaptive-concurrency rollout guard
preview: Motivation: We've observed hard 'latency cliff' failure modes where p95/p99 jump sharply within a short window even though rolling averages look stable; these events bypass percentile-based canaries and lead to partial outages. This PR implements a latency-cliff detector that detects rapid percentile deltas across s...
text: Motivation: We've observed hard 'latency cliff' failure modes where p95/p99 jump sharply within a short window even though rolling averages look stable; these events bypass percentile-based canaries and lead to partial outages. This PR implements a latency-cliff detector that detects rapid percentile deltas across short windows and wires an adaptive-concurrency rollout guard that temporarily reduces per-replica concurrency and pauses rollouts when a cliff is detected. Changes include detector algorithm, integration with the canary evaluation pipeline, a lightweight local load harness for reproducible cliffs, dashboards, and unit/integration tests. Checklist: [x] detector algorithm and unit tests, [x] canary integration and gating, [x] adaptive-concurrency policy and actuator, [x] dashboards and runbook, [x] CI flake fixes. Rollout plan: rollout as a guarded feature flag for 2 weeks, e...

title: crosslang-credential-sanity-and-stream-handoff-polish
preview: Motivation: We observed intermittent duplicate credential refreshes and subtle stream-resume races when long-lived streaming consumers migrated credentials (lease rotations) in parallel across the three official SDKs. This PR harmonizes credential lifecycles and clarifies the handoff contract for pull/push-style str...
text:
Motivation: We observed intermittent duplicate credential refreshes and subtle stream-resume races when long-lived streaming consumers migrated credentials (lease rotations) in parallel across the three official SDKs. This PR harmonizes credential lifecycles and clarifies the handoff contract for pull/push-style streams so that SDKs exhibit consistent, observable behavior under rotation and network flakiness.

What this PR does (high level):
- Introduces a small, language-agnostic contract: streams must surface a stable 'lease_id' with every chunk boundary; consumers can request a graceful handoff by calling Stream.handoff() which yields an idempotent checkpoint token. This lets client code coordinate credentials and checkpoints atomically.
- Adds a compact backoff/retry preset called "sdk:stream-sane" (adaptive jitter + capped linear) and wires it into all SDKs as an opt-in default f...

title: Add DMA stall & kernel-latency counters with sigprof-compatible timeline exporter
preview: Context: We need finer-grained, low-overhead indicators of where inference stalls occur inside the serving runtime so that model routing, batching and kernel-selection heuristics can be tuned without attaching heavy profilers. This PR adds DMA-stall counters collected from the GPU driver telemetry shim, per-kernel l...
text:
Context: We need finer-grained, low-overhead indicators of where inference stalls occur inside the serving runtime so that model routing, batching and kernel-selection heuristics can be tuned without attaching heavy profilers. This PR adds DMA-stall counters collected from the GPU driver telemetry shim, per-kernel latency histograms sampled into buckets, and a compact timeline exporter that emits SigProf-compatible records that downstream dashboards (and our internal perfboard) can ingest. Motivation: several customer incidents pointed to intermittent pauses that didn't show up in token-level latency traces (ENG-1223). Existing CPU-side sampling catches high-level stalls but misses kernel queuing and DMA scheduling hotspots.

Summary of changes (high level):
- runtime/telemetry: new dma_counters.{h,cc} implementing low-overhead atomic counters for DMA queue stalls and preemption event...

title: Contextual Hypothesis Writer and Sandboxed Fix Prover
preview:
Context and motivation:
This PR adds two coordinated features to the incident-bot suite: (1) a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that synthesizes prioritized remediation hypotheses from alert telemetry and runbook fragments, and (2) a Sandboxed Fix Prover that attempts safe, read-only or reversible dry-run fixes in ephem...
text:
Context and motivation:
This PR adds two coordinated features to the incident-bot suite: (1) a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that synthesizes prioritized remediation hypotheses from alert telemetry and runbook fragments, and (2) a Sandboxed Fix Prover that attempts safe, read-only or reversible dry-run fixes in ephemeral sandboxes to validate hypotheses before recommending operator action. The goal is to reduce mean time to mitigation for operational incidents while keeping all experiments isolated and auditable.

High-level design and behavior:
- Hypothesis Writer consumes the incident snapshot (logs, traces, metrics, recent deploys, route maps) and runbook snippets to propose 2-5 ranked hypotheses with rationale and confidence score. It attaches minimal reproduction hints and a short sequence of commands or API calls required to validate the hypothesis.
- Fix Prover executes validati...

title: Introduce jurisdictional operator claims, KMS proxy with region affinity, and audit canonicalizer
preview: Motivation: operator-initiated requests that used fallback KMS routes could violate tenant residency expectations in multi-geozone deployments. This PR introduces a lightweight KMS proxy with region-affinity routing and a jurisdictional operator-claim propagation middleware that ties RBAC v2 decisions to geozone cla...
text: Motivation: operator-initiated requests that used fallback KMS routes could violate tenant residency expectations in multi-geozone deployments. This PR introduces a lightweight KMS proxy with region-affinity routing and a jurisdictional operator-claim propagation middleware that ties RBAC v2 decisions to geozone claims. It also adds an audit header canonicalizer to ensure consistent downstream proofs for compliance ingestion. Changes include a claim backfill migration for existing operator sessions and telemetry for residency decision sampling. Checklist: - [x] design doc and Linear ticket ENG-4521 linked, - [x] RBAC v2 policy and policy tests, - [x] KMS proxy and unit tests, - [x] migration/backfill job and smoke tests, - [x] staged rollout flag + installer hook, - [ ] manual staging canary and runbook signoff. Design references: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-4521 and internal...

title: add-siesta-presubmit-prober-and-dependency-visibility-index
preview:
Context:
We have growing CI wall time from running monolithic integration matrices for small changes. This PR introduces a lightweight presubmit prober (codename: Siesta) and a dependency visibility index to reduce premerge feedback time. The prober is intentionally minimal: static-source heuristics, quick-path test...
text:
Context:
We have growing CI wall time from running monolithic integration matrices for small changes. This PR introduces a lightweight presubmit prober (codename: Siesta) and a dependency visibility index to reduce premerge feedback time. The prober is intentionally minimal: static-source heuristics, quick-path test selectors, and a small Go probe binary that emits a deterministic 'presubmit fingerprint' for changed paths.

Motivation:
- Reduce developer wait times on common doc/SDK/infra tweaks by avoiding full integration runs unless needed.
- Provide a reproducible preflight check that can be run locally and in CI to gate expensive shards.
- Make dependency visibility explicit so presubmit selection is conservatively correct for transitive changes.

What this PR does (summary):
- Adds a new lightweight Go binary cmd/presubmit-prober (approx 120 LOC) that scans a change list and com...

title: rollout-conductor-introduce-contract-lanes-and-playbooked-rollback
preview: Motivation: Multiple teams have been extending canary and shadow runtimes with ad-hoc safety checks and manual rollback runbooks, which leads to inconsistent behavior across Dedicated and Private deployments. This PR introduces a rollout conductor that formalizes 'contract lanes' - named SLO/contract driven lanes th...
text: Motivation: Multiple teams have been extending canary and shadow runtimes with ad-hoc safety checks and manual rollback runbooks, which leads to inconsistent behavior across Dedicated and Private deployments. This PR introduces a rollout conductor that formalizes 'contract lanes' - named SLO/contract driven lanes that encode metric gates, degradation policy, and automated playbooked rollback steps. The goal is to provide a single declarative place to define lifecycle behavior for canary/A-B/shadow experiments, allow per-customer overrides in Dedicated, and enable safe automated rollback with human-in-loop confirmation when required. What this changes: 1) a new CRD schema for RolloutContract and ContractLane (helm/templates/rollout-contract-crd.yaml), 2) a conductor service that evaluates lane gates against streaming metrics and traces and emits actions (services/rollout/conductor.go, ...

title: multi-tool-execution: ergonomic API + parallel tool runner + result injection + retry policies
preview: Motivation: Improve ergonomics and reliability for tool/function-calling in the OpenAI-compat layer and native Redwood API. Customers asked for easy multi-tool invocations, deterministic injection of tool results into continuations, safe parallel tool execution, and more robust retry/backoff for transient tool failu...
text: Motivation: Improve ergonomics and reliability for tool/function-calling in the OpenAI-compat layer and native Redwood API. Customers asked for easy multi-tool invocations, deterministic injection of tool results into continuations, safe parallel tool execution, and more robust retry/backoff for transient tool failures. This PR introduces a small, backwards-compatible API extension plus a new parallel tool-runner implementation and runtime wiring for result injection and structured output hints. Summary of changes: 1) API: add `tools` invocation signature that supports multiple tool calls in a single step and an optional `parallel` flag. 2) Runtime: implement ParallelToolRunner with optimistic concurrency, coordinated token streaming, and ordered result injection points. 3) Retry semantics: add configurable retry/backoff policies per-tool with circuit-breaking thresholds and idempoten...

title: elastic-wait-slope-scheduler-with-proactive-kv-hinting
preview: Context: In high-concurrency continuous-batching workloads we observed head-of-line effects where a small number of long KV-miss-heavy requests cause repeated batch hold-ups and elevated p99. Motivation: Provide a scheduler heuristic that favors low-wait-slope requests (likely short or cache-hit dominated) to reduce...
text: Context: In high-concurrency continuous-batching workloads we observed head-of-line effects where a small number of long KV-miss-heavy requests cause repeated batch hold-ups and elevated p99. Motivation: Provide a scheduler heuristic that favors low-wait-slope requests (likely short or cache-hit dominated) to reduce p99 tail while ensuring starvation-free progress for heavy requests. Design: Introduce an 'elastic wait-slope' metric computed per request as recent wait-time divided by estimated service tokens; the admission controller prefers requests with low wait-slope but maintains a dynamically-sized 'elastic window' that guarantees periodic admission of higher-slope requests. To prevent livelock we add a per-queue aging credit that increases admission priority linearly with time and is bounded by the window manager. KV cache integration: A lightweight prefetch hinter predicts likel...

title: cyclic-attn: lane-stride rotary-shift fusion with loopback prefetcher
preview: Motivation: reduce per-token latency for short-to-mid sequences and improve throughput for bursty, heterogeneous workloads by reducing memory traffic and improving kernel L2 reuse. This PR introduces a 'cyclic lane-stride' attention microkernel that fuses rotary/rope shift operations into the qk/v compute path and a...
text:
Motivation: reduce per-token latency for short-to-mid sequences and improve throughput for bursty, heterogeneous workloads by reducing memory traffic and improving kernel L2 reuse. This PR introduces a 'cyclic lane-stride' attention microkernel that fuses rotary/rope shift operations into the qk/v compute path and adds a small on-chip loopback prefetcher to warm the KV cache lanes during continuous batching.

Summary of changes: fused rotary-shift pass merged into the QK kernel to avoid standalone rotary transposes; introduced lane-stride tiling to improve SIMD coalescing on Ampere/Blackwell-class GPUs; added a lightweight loopback prefetcher that issues speculative KV loads for the next contiguous lane during continuous batching; extended the KV cache API with an "anticipated_lanes" hint used by the runtime scheduler for warmup; scheduler updates to support cyclic assignment of reque...

title: Ensemble headroom calibrator and burst quieting for smoother autoscaling
preview:
Background and motivation:
Services using Dedicated fleets exhibit frequent autoscaler churning when tenant traffic arrives as short high-frequency microbursts. The existing single-signal headroom estimator (90th-percentile EWMA) under-reacts to transient demand and over-reacts when bursts happen repeatedly, produci...
text:
Background and motivation:
Services using Dedicated fleets exhibit frequent autoscaler churning when tenant traffic arrives as short high-frequency microbursts. The existing single-signal headroom estimator (90th-percentile EWMA) under-reacts to transient demand and over-reacts when bursts happen repeatedly, producing oscillation. This PR implements two complementary mechanisms: 1) an ensemble headroom calibrator that combines multiple short- and medium-horizon predictors to produce a more stable headroom suggestion, and 2) a burst-quieting cooldown adapter that temporarily suppresses aggressive autoscaler decommits when microburst patterns are detected.

High-level summary of changes:
- runtime/autoscaler/ensemble_calibrator.go: New ensemble estimator that fuses EWMA, short AR(1) predictor, and a quantile sketch (P2 approximation) into a weighted headroom suggestion per-tenant. The w...

title: dynamic per-route payload limits + streaming proxy buffer tuning to stabilize function-calls and OpenAI-compatible chunking
preview: Motivation: Several production routes (chat-stream, tools/function_call, and OpenAI-compat proxying) have experienced intermittent stalls and truncated function_call payloads when large intermediate tool outputs are streamed in small chunks. Root cause summary: a) statically-sized streaming proxy flush thresholds an...
text:
Motivation: Several production routes (chat-stream, tools/function_call, and OpenAI-compat proxying) have experienced intermittent stalls and truncated function_call payloads when large intermediate tool outputs are streamed in small chunks. Root cause summary: a) statically-sized streaming proxy flush thresholds and buffer headroom do not match per-route payload patterns; b) global payload size limit was too coarse (caused safe-failures for tool outputs but allowed oversized non-streamed requests); c) OpenAI compatibility shim assumed final-message framing which breaks when intermediate buffer flushes split function_call JSON tokens across frames.

What this PR does (summary): Adds a dynamic per-route payload limiter and adaptive streaming-proxy buffer tuning. The gateway now derives two parameters per route: effective_payload_limit (bytes) and buffer_flush_threshold (bytes). Derivat...

title: regional-kv-pause-and-softbackoff-guard-hotfix
preview: Summary: Hotfix to reduce region-wide KV eviction storms that were causing transient SLO breaches during metadata-thrashing incidents. Root cause: under high regional egress the KV sticky-preserve heuristic allowed aggressive KV retention eviction loops that amplified load; eviction churn caused higher CPU on the se...
text: Summary: Hotfix to reduce region-wide KV eviction storms that were causing transient SLO breaches during metadata-thrashing incidents. Root cause: under high regional egress the KV sticky-preserve heuristic allowed aggressive KV retention eviction loops that amplified load; eviction churn caused higher CPU on the serving runtime and led to regional retry storms. This PR introduces a defensive 'regional KV pause' gate and an exponential soft-backoff for sticky eviction decisions, plus extra telemetry and an emergency runbook link. Motivation and context: after incident SUP-9012 on 2026-02-18 we observed repeated reopen cycles tied to KV pressure; prior mitigations (short-term drain) prevented immediate outage but did not stop churn. The fix is intentionally conservative and primarily runtime-side so it can be backported to release branches. Changes in this PR: 1) runtime/eviction.go: i...

title: cross-sdk-compat-layer-and-ergonomic-stream-consumer-batching
preview: Context: Multiple teams requested consistent streaming ergonomics and auth/reauth behavior across the three official SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go). This PR implements a lightweight compatibility layer in the monorepo to centralize retry profiles, stream consumer semantics, and signal/shutdown handling so SDKs can conv...
text: Context: Multiple teams requested consistent streaming ergonomics and auth/reauth behavior across the three official SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go). This PR implements a lightweight compatibility layer in the monorepo to centralize retry profiles, stream consumer semantics, and signal/shutdown handling so SDKs can converge without duplicating policy logic. Motivation: parity gaps were causing subtle production regressions (different backoff curves, resume semantics, and platform shutdown behavior). Goals: 1) provide a single source of truth for retry/backoff presets used by all SDKs, 2) standardize streaming consumer APIs (async iterator in Python, async generator / AsyncIterable in TS, context-aware streamer in Go) and 3) add platform-specific shutdown handling (improved Windows Ctrl-C/signal handling) and a small set of batching presets for low-latency / high-throughput workloads. Summ...

title: Introduce 'Thrifty' inference profile generator for conservative batching/caching/quant presets
preview: Adds a new service+CLI to synthesize conservative, production-safe default inference profiles from short trace samples. Motivation: many customers hesitate to enable aggressive batching/quantization/caching because they lack templates that balance unit-economics wins with latency/regression risk. This change introdu...
text: Adds a new service+CLI to synthesize conservative, production-safe default inference profiles from short trace samples. Motivation: many customers hesitate to enable aggressive batching/quantization/caching because they lack templates that balance unit-economics wins with latency/regression risk. This change introduces 'thrifty' profiles that err on the side of lower cost while preserving tail-latency SLOs. Key pieces: 1) trace-sampler: lightweight offline analyzer that ingests representative request traces and outputs distribution summaries (seq len, token mix, prefix reuse). 2) profile-generator: rules engine that maps trace summaries to a prioritized preset (batch size, prefetch window, prefix cache policy, int8/4 quantization candidate with safety fences). 3) rw-profile-gen CLI and Docker image for customers to run locally or in infra pipelines. 4) integration with recommendations...

title: Introduce influence-matrix eval hook and rollback scorer
preview: Motivation: We've had recurring incidents where a small prompt tweak in a PR produced outsized regressions for a narrow but critical slice of prompt-sets. Existing CI smokechecks flag macro regressions but provide little signal about which prompts, tokens, or candidate edits are most influential. This PR adds an 'in...
text:
Motivation: We've had recurring incidents where a small prompt tweak in a PR produced outsized regressions for a narrow but critical slice of prompt-sets. Existing CI smokechecks flag macro regressions but provide little signal about which prompts, tokens, or candidate edits are most influential. This PR adds an 'influence matrix' eval hook that attributes per-prompt and per-perturbation influence on aggregate metrics, and a lightweight rollback scorer that suggests the smallest prompt change(s) that would return metrics to baseline.

What this does: Implements an eval hook that runs alongside existing regression checks. For each prompt-set it computes an influence score per prompt and per change vector using a randomized leave-k-out approximation (bounded sampling plus importance-weighting) and aggregates into an influence matrix (prompt x perturbation). The CI adapter consumes aggre...

title: Add invocation cohorting and kernel-warmup heatmap with adaptive alert backoff
preview: Motivation: We see repeated noisy alerts driven by small cohorts of invocations (odd prompt shapes or very long KV cache cold starts). Teams need a way to attribute cost/latency anomalies to invocation cohorts and to visualize how kernel latency evolves across the first N tokens as kernels and KV caches warm up. Thi...
text: Motivation: We see repeated noisy alerts driven by small cohorts of invocations (odd prompt shapes or very long KV cache cold starts). Teams need a way to attribute cost/latency anomalies to invocation cohorts and to visualize how kernel latency evolves across the first N tokens as kernels and KV caches warm up. This PR introduces: 1) a lightweight per-invocation cohorting tag (invocation_cohort_id) based on prompt-shape and context-warmth heuristics; 2) a kernel-warmup heatmap that aggregates kernel-level latency for the first 32 tokens vs KV-warmth buckets and exposes a Grafana dashboard; 3) an adaptive alert-backoff rule that quiets alerts for cohorts that show high variance but low long-term impact, and increases sampling and digesting instead of immediate paging. Checklist: - [x] Add cohort id generation and propagation into traces and metrics - [x] Emit cohort-level rollup count...

title: accrual-ledger-for-tenant-bursting-and-thresholded-refill
preview:
Context and motivation:
This PR introduces an 'accrual ledger' for Dedicated fleets: a lightweight per-tenant credit accounting system that lets tenants accumulate unused capacity as credits and spend them for controlled bursts later. Motivation came from multiple customers whose bursty workloads either starved othe...
text:
Context and motivation:
This PR introduces an 'accrual ledger' for Dedicated fleets: a lightweight per-tenant credit accounting system that lets tenants accumulate unused capacity as credits and spend them for controlled bursts later. Motivation came from multiple customers whose bursty workloads either starved other tenants or caused over-provisioning. Instead of hard reservation or coarse burst tokens, the accrual approach provides fine-grained economics-aware bursting with safety fences.

Design highlights:
- AccrualKeeper component (control-plane): keeps an EWMA-based balance per tenant and enforces a max-credit cap. Credits accrue when tenant utilization is below a configurable target window (default: 5m EWMA w/ 30th percentile headroom).
- Thresholded refill: when a tenant's credit drops below refill_threshold%, the system will gradually grant refill tokens from a dedicated warm...

title: Introduce auditor-proxy RBAC role and KMS failover shim with region-pin rewrite
preview: Motivation: Several enterprise customers and our compliance team requested a hardened, auditable read-only access path for internal auditors and third-party compliance reviewers that does not increase the blast radius nor allow data export. This PR introduces an auditor-proxy RBAC role and a KMS failover shim that e...
text:
Motivation: Several enterprise customers and our compliance team requested a hardened, auditable read-only access path for internal auditors and third-party compliance reviewers that does not increase the blast radius nor allow data export. This PR introduces an auditor-proxy RBAC role and a KMS failover shim that enforces region-pin rewrite semantics for audit reads so that decryption is always bound to the data residency constraints.

Scope: Single PR implementing RBAC v2 policy artifacts, an audit-forwarder shim that mediates auditor requests, a KMS failover path that supports per-request region pin rewriting, console UI for granting the auditor-proxy role, and Terraform bindings to create the minimal IAM resources. The feature is feature-flagged behind enable_auditor_proxy.

What changed (high level):
- New RBAC policy: auditor_proxy_v1 (read-only, no-export guardrails, session-le...

title: weighted-queue-and-kv-pin-timeouts-to-mitigate-starvation
preview: Context: Production traces and perf canaries exposed recurring tail-latency regressions under mixed workloads with hot and cold tenants. Root cause analysis showed that the continuous batching scheduler's FIFO-dominant queue allowed small high-priority requests to be repeatedly overtaken when the runtime favored lar...
text: Context: Production traces and perf canaries exposed recurring tail-latency regressions under mixed workloads with hot and cold tenants. Root cause analysis showed that the continuous batching scheduler's FIFO-dominant queue allowed small high-priority requests to be repeatedly overtaken when the runtime favored large batched prefixes; simultaneously the KV cache had a long pin expiry that amplified hot-key monopolization. This PR introduces two coordinated changes: 1) a lightweight weighted-queue throttler in the continuous batcher that enforces service-share weights at queue admission time (soft scheduling), and 2) adjustable KV-cache pin timeouts and a small-metadata eviction hint to reduce long-running pin monopolies. Design: - Admissions now record a per-queue weight token and a sliding-window deficit counter; long-running peers lose admission weight over configurable windows to ...

title: Introduce presubmit fast-cache + dev-dep visibility adapter
preview: Motivation: CI presubmit lanes have regressed in median feedback time due to cold CI images and noisy dev-dependency churn. This PR introduces a presubmit "fast-cache" layer for our CI runner image and a small dependency-visibility adapter that enforces an explicit dev-dep manifest to prevent accidental transitive d...
text:
Motivation: CI presubmit lanes have regressed in median feedback time due to cold CI images and noisy dev-dependency churn. This PR introduces a presubmit "fast-cache" layer for our CI runner image and a small dependency-visibility adapter that enforces an explicit dev-dep manifest to prevent accidental transitive dev-dependency installs.

Goals: 1) reduce end-to-end presubmit feedback time for lint/test jobs by warming a lightweight artifact cache (node_modules tarballs, pip wheels) scoped to the PR diff; 2) make dev-dependency changes explicit so CI cache invalidation is more intentional and less noisy; 3) avoid large monolithic CI image rebuilds on each dev-dep bump.

What this changes: - new CI helper: .github/presubmit/cache-warmup.sh (shell helper run in presubmit lanes to warm cache from fast-cache service) - presubmit fast-cache service client: infra/ci/fastcache/ (minimal go ...

title: introduce-metric-dependency-graph-and-alert-orchestrator
preview: Motivation: We frequently see noisy correlated signals where a downstream alert creates a cascade of symptom alerts and noisy paging. This PR introduces a lightweight metric dependency graph and an alert orchestrator that uses that graph to: 1) compute transitive impact scores for alerts, 2) suppress or de-duplicate...
text: Motivation: We frequently see noisy correlated signals where a downstream alert creates a cascade of symptom alerts and noisy paging. This PR introduces a lightweight metric dependency graph and an alert orchestrator that uses that graph to: 1) compute transitive impact scores for alerts, 2) suppress or de-duplicate follow-on alerts for 60s by default, and 3) publish derived metrics (root-cause-likelihood, alert-impact-score) for dashboards and SLO decomposition. Design summary: - graph_builder: constructs a directed weighted graph from time-series cross-correlation and historical co-incident alert windows. We persist the graph as a compact adjacency list and per-edge confidence scores. - orchestrator: a service that subscribes to alert events and consults the graph to decide suppression, priority escalation, and annotated alert payloads. - derived metrics: we emit root_cause_prob and...

title: oncall handoff checksum and telemetry synthesizer for compact context transfer
preview: Motivation: handoffs during high-pressure incidents frequently lost critical context (partial traces, clipped logs, ad-hoc snippets). This PR adds two complementary features to reduce costly context gaps: 1) a deterministic handoff checksum attached to every incident-bot handoff message that verifies payload fidelit...
text: Motivation: handoffs during high-pressure incidents frequently lost critical context (partial traces, clipped logs, ad-hoc snippets). This PR adds two complementary features to reduce costly context gaps: 1) a deterministic handoff checksum attached to every incident-bot handoff message that verifies payload fidelity across tools; 2) a telemetry synthesizer service that constructs a compact, privacy-safe summary of missing spans/events when downstream telemetry is sparse. The checksum is a canonical JSON serialization + SHA256 digest with stable ordering and configurable salt, surfaced as 'handoff_checksum' in handoff payloads and displayed in the oncall UI; operators can use it to assert the handoff message received by downstream runbooks/Slack threads matches the sent content. The telemetry synthesizer runs as an opt-in stage during handoff: it inspects available traces/logs, infers...

title: Session-pinned canary with deterministic shadow replay and graceful drain
preview: Motivation: reduce noisy user-visible regressions during canary promotions by ensuring session-affine traffic stays pinned while enabling deterministic shadow replay for offline verification before promotion. This PR implements a session-pinned canary lane that pins user sessions to the incumbent variant until a ses...
text: Motivation: reduce noisy user-visible regressions during canary promotions by ensuring session-affine traffic stays pinned while enabling deterministic shadow replay for offline verification before promotion. This PR implements a session-pinned canary lane that pins user sessions to the incumbent variant until a session-safe handoff completes and introduces a deterministic shadow replay path that replays sampled live requests into candidate models with preserved session state. Key goals: maintain session consistency for stateful flows, provide reproducible shadow results for automated regression detectors, and support graceful drain with end-to-end idempotency guarantees. Design summary: 1) Session affinity tokens: requests now carry a stable session token used by the canary conductor to decide pinning eligibility. 2) Handoff protocol: a three-phase handshake (prepare, verify, commit)...

title: Circuit stabilizer: connection quieting and tenant SLO-tilt hotpatch
preview: Motivation: During an incident on 2026-02-14 we observed rapid circuit reopen/reclose oscillations for certain islanded regions which amplified load and caused tenant-level SLO violations. Root cause analysis showed two compounding issues: (1) the circuit reconciler could race when reconciling restored health probes...
text: Motivation: During an incident on 2026-02-14 we observed rapid circuit reopen/reclose oscillations for certain islanded regions which amplified load and caused tenant-level SLO violations. Root cause analysis showed two compounding issues: (1) the circuit reconciler could race when reconciling restored health probes and reopen attempts, and (2) the shedder decision logic treated all SLO deficits equally, causing aggressive shedding for tenants where small latency increases were acceptable and for others where impact was severe. This PR introduces a targeted hotpatch that (a) adds a short 'connection quieting' window after a circuit state change to prevent immediate reopen retries, (b) introduces a tenant SLO-tilt score that biases the regional shedder away from high-impact tenants when feasible, and (c) fixes the reconcilation race by serializing state transitions through a single rec...

title: Commitment signal multiplexer and safe throttle proxy
preview: Motivation: We see recurring incidents where autoscaler decisions diverge from external commitment inputs (billing/reservation overrides, SRE manual patches, and predictive headroom hints), causing transient overcommit or overly-conservative throttles. This PR introduces a Commitment Signal Multiplexer and a Safe Th...
text: Motivation: We see recurring incidents where autoscaler decisions diverge from external commitment inputs (billing/reservation overrides, SRE manual patches, and predictive headroom hints), causing transient overcommit or overly-conservative throttles. This PR introduces a Commitment Signal Multiplexer and a Safe Throttle Proxy to 1) consolidate multiple commitment sources into a single normalized commitment feed, 2) apply conservative safety rules and smoothing for downstream autoscalers and per-tenant throttle enforcement, and 3) provide observability for reconciliation reasoning. High-level changes: - pkg/autoscaler/commitment_mux: new multiplexer that ingests: reservation ledger commits, predictive headroom hints, manual overrides (Linear/console), and observed utilization signal. It publishes a time-series normalized_commitment{tenant} with provenance tags and confidence scores. ...

title: lease-based-idempotency-and-adaptive-retry-window-in-openai-adapter
preview:
Context and motivation:
We keep seeing customer production failures where naive client retries (especially across streaming tool-invocations) either trigger duplicate side-effects or fail to respect per-customer transient capacity limits. Previous effort focused on clarifying 429 semantics and idempotency key format...
text:
Context and motivation:
We keep seeing customer production failures where naive client retries (especially across streaming tool-invocations) either trigger duplicate side-effects or fail to respect per-customer transient capacity limits. Previous effort focused on clarifying 429 semantics and idempotency key formats, but two pain points remain: (1) idempotency keys are long-lived and encourage reuse across logically distinct operations, and (2) the server's "Retry-After" hints are either too sparse or ambiguous for streaming/tool-call flows.

This PR implements two coordinated server-side changes in the OpenAI compatibility layer and the toolkit used by tool-calling/runbook integrations: (A) lease-based idempotency tokens (short-lived, single-use leases that carry scope and validation metadata), and (B) an adaptive retry-window envelope returned in a structured response header/frame ...

title: introduce-ecohistorian-batch-indexer-and-defaults-generator
preview: Motivation: We repeatedly see suboptimal tenant defaults for batching and cache sizing because current heuristics don't leverage historical batch shapes and cross-tenant cache hit signals. This PR adds an offline "ecohistorian" indexer service that ingests anonymized serving telemetry and produces tenant-specific ca...
text:
Motivation: We repeatedly see suboptimal tenant defaults for batching and cache sizing because current heuristics don't leverage historical batch shapes and cross-tenant cache hit signals. This PR adds an offline "ecohistorian" indexer service that ingests anonymized serving telemetry and produces tenant-specific candidate defaults (batch shape templates, KV cache size suggestions, and conservative quantization hints). The goal is automation: feed the optimizer with statistically grounded defaults instead of hand-tuned heuristics.

What this PR does (summary): 1) New service pkg/ecohistorian: a lightweight indexer that summarizes sequence-length distributions, inter-request spacing, prefix reuse, and cache hit rates into compact signatures. 2) Generator module in tools/econ-defaults: consumes historian signatures to output tenant defaults as YAML/JSON (batching.default, cache.size_hin...

title: Add stochastic concurrency fence and staggered probe scheduler to reduce probe-induced noise
preview: Context and motivation: We observed a recurring class of false-positive canary regressions when probe traffic arrives in short bursts or when client-side concurrency spikes align with canary probe windows. These cases amplify tail latency and create high variance in the canary signal, causing unnecessary rollbacks a...
text:
Context and motivation: We observed a recurring class of false-positive canary regressions when probe traffic arrives in short bursts or when client-side concurrency spikes align with canary probe windows. These cases amplify tail latency and create high variance in the canary signal, causing unnecessary rollbacks and developer churn. This PR implements two complementary mitigations: a stochastic concurrency fence (probabilistic admission control) to smooth bursty concurrent requests and a staggered probe scheduler to desynchronize probe timing across hosts.

What this changes (high level):
- Add perf-canary detector that computes `concurrency_entropy` per host and enables a probabilistic cap when entropy exceeds threshold.
- Replace existing probe dispatcher with a staggered scheduler that shards probe time windows to hosts and backfills missed probes without amplifying traffic.
- Ex...

title: elastic-deadline-smoothing-with-occupancy-crediting-and-kv-prefetch-hysteresis
preview:
Motivation:
High-variance arrival patterns combined with KV warmups produce occasional tail spikes in the continuous batcher even when average utilization is healthy. Our SLOs are sensitive to these tails for interactive endpoints. This change introduces an \"elastic deadline smoothing\" layer in the scheduler that ...
text:
Motivation:
High-variance arrival patterns combined with KV warmups produce occasional tail spikes in the continuous batcher even when average utilization is healthy. Our SLOs are sensitive to these tails for interactive endpoints. This change introduces an \"elastic deadline smoothing\" layer in the scheduler that smooths slot admission deadlines across short horizons and a complementary \"occupancy crediting\" mechanism that temporarily increases admission priority for lanes that have been starved. In addition, the KV prefetcher was augmented with a hysteresis window to avoid synchronized prefetch storms during cold-starts.

What this PR does (summary):
- Adds serving/scheduler/elastic_deadline_smoother.rs: a scheduler stage that maintains a short rolling deadline histogram and applies a smoothing kernel (exponential decay) to outgoing admission deadlines to avoid sharp deadline osc...
